page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by Intel processors. It features an x86_64 architecture with 192 physical cores and 384 virtual CPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system provides dedicated CPU allocation with 2.0 threads per core.

The server is equipped with 1536.0 GB of memory, maintaining a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, it includes 22800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-capacity, low-latency local storage directly attached to the bare-metal instance.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This server does not include any graphics processing units. The combination of high-density compute, large memory capacity, and extensive local NVMe storage makes this instance appropriate for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and enterprise-level general-purpose workloads requiring direct hardware access.

This instance does not contain any graphics processing units. The high resource density and massive local storage capacity make this bare-metal server suitable for large-scale database deployments, enterprise applications, and data-intensive general-purpose workloads.
